# Govulncheck triage allowlist.
# Keep each GO-* advisory ID at the beginning of the line: .github/workflows/security.yml parses this file.
# Full owner/severity/mitigation/milestone records live in docs/security/security-gates-triage.md.
# New GO-* IDs from the Security Gate workflow are untriaged and must fail PRs until added with full triage.
#
# Re-triaged 2026-07-09 against a live `govulncheck ./...` run (default reachability mode,
# the same mode this repo's CI parses). Only advisories that actually appear as a reachable
# "Vulnerability #" finding need an entry here for CI to pass; govulncheck's default mode
# does not surface the larger "imported but never called" set at all (confirmed: 16 such
# advisories are present in the dependency tree but produce no "Vulnerability #" line and
# never require an allowlist entry). The 2026-07-09 run found only 3 reachable findings;
# every other ID below is kept only as a defensive/historical record in case an older or
# future govulncheck run resurfaces it (e.g. after a temporary dependency downgrade).
GO-2023-1821 owner=security severity=high impact=dependency milestone=public-testnet-gate mitigation=track-upstream-upgrade status=triaged
GO-2023-1881 owner=security severity=high impact=dependency milestone=public-testnet-gate mitigation=track-upstream-upgrade status=triaged
GO-2024-2584 owner=security severity=high impact=consensus-adjacent-dependency milestone=public-testnet-gate mitigation=false-positive-fix-already-present-in-pinned-v0.54.3-stale-osv-range status=fixed
GO-2025-3442 owner=security severity=high impact=dependency milestone=public-testnet-gate mitigation=track-upstream-upgrade status=triaged
